raw_consumer_statement:
I placed an order for an item that was to be delivered in 10-20 days. This was a standard item ( a XXXXXXXX XXXX desk organizer ) that was billed at a price about half of other companies. I was immediately charged {$25.00} on a XXXX account which I had charged to my American Express account charge was made on XX/XX/2023. When myAmex statement arrived showing the charge I disputed it ( on XXXX ) as I never received any confirmation from the purported " seller '' nor have I have of of this date received the item I ordered. American Express closed the dispute on the bizarre basis that charge was justified with no explanation provided. I have demanded the charge be reopened as of even date. 

Any preliminary investigation would have determined that the immediate transfer of an Amex charge to XXXX which I never authorizes, the failure of seller to ever confirm the order and the failure to deliver merchandise after near 2 months is transparently fraudulent. I am entitled to a credit for {$25.00} and a recognition by Amex of its failure to comply with applicable law.
